title: stevia jorullensis var. ehrenbergiana
intro: Stevia jorullensis var. ehrenbergiana, also known as Stevia ehrenbergiana, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Mexico and Central America. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are known for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, and have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to lower blood pressure, reduce blood sugar levels, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. Stevia ehrenbergiana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and its herbal preparations are often used to promote overall health and well-being, particularly in the treatment of hypertension and diabetes.
